hero_subtitle: SFTY helps FIU teams consolidate digital asset alerts, evidence, investigations and reporting into a unified supervisory workflow built for transparent case handling.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market

problem_title: AML investigations slow without connected cases
problem_description: FIU teams struggle to link alerts, evidence and reporting across fragmented systems. This scattering delays investigation workflows and weakens audit readiness in suspicious transaction reviews for digital assets.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY organizes fragmented alerts, wallet activity, VASP data and investigative evidence into one linked case workflow, enabling FIU teams to manage reviews and reporting with clear audit trails.
workflow_title: From alerts to case investigation and reporting
workflow_description: SFTY channels digital asset alerts through structured case creation, investigative review, evidence logging and supervisory reporting within a secure regulatory workflow.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One case-based supervision
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented alerts, investigations, evidence and reporting into a structured case workflow designed specifically for FIU digital asset supervision needs.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides regulators a secure environment with controlled access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-safe deployment for rigorous AML case management workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how to manage AML case workflows securely
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how FIU teams structure digital asset investigation cases
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports AML case reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe supervisory workflows for managing suspicious activity reports
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for FIU and AML teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides FIU teams with a connected platform to connect alerts, investigations, entity data, cases and reporting for structured digital asset AML supervision and intelligence workflows.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY manage AML case workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures AML alerts into cases with linked entity profiles, transaction data, evidence, decision logs and reporting to support investigation and regulatory workflows in one environment.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve case evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves investigation history, decision logs and supporting evidence to maintain an auditable record for supervisory review and reporting readiness throughout the AML case lifecycle.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government stacks?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, KYC, KYT and Travel Rule providers while enforcing role-based access, audit logs and secure deployment controls.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing AML analytics tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates as a supervisory layer connecting multiple provider outputs into structured workflows without replacing underlying AML or analytics solutions used by authorities.
final_title: Structure AML cases into one controlled workflow
final_description: Review how SFTY helps FIU teams organize alerts, investigations, evidence and reporting into connected supervisory cases.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
